response.justification: Strong senior generalist with notable geospatial (GISCollective, Green Map) and low-latency distributed systems experience (Sociomantic, millions of HTTP req/s), but the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) and the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015. No Rust and no navigation/routing pipeline experience. DLang background suggests ability to ramp on systems-level work, but this is a meaningful skills gap for a mid-senior IC role centered on C++.
strengths: Founder-level geospatial/GIS experience directly relevant to map data domain
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic using async I/O and fibers
Senior engineer with strong fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, Linux, multi-language fluency)
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months, 2014-2015); no Rust
No prior work on navigation, routing, or map tiling pipelines specifically
Recent stack is web-centric (TypeScript/Node), not systems C++ on Linux servers
